nav.modePrayDesc: Read daily prayers and sacred texts
nav.modeKeepDayDesc: Follow the season, feast, and observance

hero.title: Your daily church companion
hero.tagline: Prayer, hymns, and the Ethiopian Orthodox calendar - gathered in one quiet place.

today.title: What the Church is keeping
today.cta: Open full calendar & Today in Church

gateway.title: Enter through learning, prayer, or the day itself.
gateway.deck: After the hero, the product should quickly orient you. Choose the mode that matches what you need now.
gateway.practiceBody: Practice mezmur, rhythm, and movement with a more guided studio flow.
gateway.prayersBody: Enter sacred reading with calmer prayer surfaces and shorter paths into the text.
gateway.calendarBody: See why today matters through feast, season, observance, and practical next steps.

section.title: The day the Church is keeping
section.deck: Calendar snapshot - tap a date in the mini month to explore that day, or open a section below.
section.newHere: New here? Read about the site

page.selectDay: Select a day to view observance details.

upcoming.helper: Civil-calendar order when an anchor exists; otherwise the clearest data window. Confirm every date with parish books.
upcoming.openDay: Open this day in the calendar

today.title: Today's feast or commemoration
today.regularDaySummary: Continue with daily prayer and remembrance.
today.noWeeklyFast: No standing Wednesday/Friday fast on this weekday.
today.notesLead: A pastoral line from the snapshot - confirm every fast and feast with your parish calendar and spiritual father.

seasonPanel.noSeasonalFast: No major seasonal fast in this window - confirm yearly with your parish.

hero.description: Mezmur, werb, and liturgical hymns - focused practice. For prayer texts and tselot, use Daily Prayers.

intro.openText: Open the hymn library and start with one piece.
intro.memorizeText: Use shorter lyric and rhythm passes before full repetition.
intro.practiceText: Return to the same hymn through the week for deeper learning.

library.helper: Browse Ethiopian Orthodox hymns and practice with lyrics, videos, and learning tools.
library.previewDeck: A short set of chants with video when available. Open one to practice with lyrics side by side.
library.libraryNote: chants in the library - filters and search appear when you browse all.
library.noMatchDeck: Nothing in the library matched this search. You can still open the same practice tools with a YouTube link.

custom.drawerDeck: Paste one video link. Lyrics, loops, and recording work the same as for library chants - optional fields below only help the learning panel.
custom.lyricsPlaceholder: Ge'ez or primary script - paste if you have them.
custom.notesPlaceholder: Context, feast day, or reminders for the learning panel.
custom.errMissing: Paste a YouTube link or video ID to begin.
custom.errInvalid: That link does not look like a valid YouTube video. Try a watch URL, youtu.be link, Shorts, embed, or the 11-character video ID.

loop.title: Loop practice
loop.autoHint: Auto split is the default practice flow. Choose a section and replay it.
loop.empty: No saved loops yet. Mark start and end, then use Save loop.
loop.saveThisRange: Save this range as a new loop
loop.errorMarkBoth: Mark both start and end first.
loop.errorEndAfterStart: End must be after start by at least a moment.
loop.errorMarkBothSave: Mark both start and end before saving.
loop.errorEndAfterStartSave: End must be after start before saving.
loop.errorMaxSaved: You can save up to 30 loops per chant.
loop.errorVideoNotReady: Video length not ready yet. Try again in a moment.
loop.errorAutoSplitShort: Auto split is unavailable for very short videos. Use Advanced Loop Practice.

player.noVideo: No YouTube link is set for this chant. Lyrics are still available beside this panel. Video is not available for this mezmur yet.
player.memoryDescription: Practice reciting {title} from memory. The lyrics are hidden so you can listen and recall without reading.
player.memoryHint: Switch to "With Lyrics" mode if you need to review the text.
player.notFoundDeck: This direct link does not match a mezmur in the Practice library.
player.metaDescription: Practice {title} with lyrics and video on Tewahedo Daily.

tabs.bothModeHint: Switch to Ge'ez or transliteration only for line-by-line study tools.
tabs.noTransliteration: Transliteration is not available for this chant.

memory.title: Memorization
memory.tip1: Read one line aloud several times before adding the next.
memory.tip2: Listen at slower speed, then speak with the recording.
memory.tip3: Use first-letter hints only as a bridge - return to full words often.
memory.tip4: In church, follow the cantor and keep eyes lifted toward the holy icons when appropriate.

recording.title: Practice Recording
recording.unsupported: Voice recording is not supported in this browser.

miniGuide.openText: Open the hymn library and start with one piece.
miniGuide.memorizeText: Use shorter lyric and rhythm passes before full repetition.
miniGuide.practiceText: Return to the same hymn through the week for deeper learning.

hero.title: Prayer collections
hero.description: Open a prayer book first, then choose its chapter, section, or prayer.

search.label: Search all prayers
search.placeholder: Search title, collection section, or text
search.emptyHint: Try a shorter search or a collection name.
search.secondaryTagsHelper: Tags such as {firstTag}, Mary, repentance, liturgical, saints, and feasts remain available inside search results and prayer pages, but the main structure is organized by prayer collection.

collection.label: Prayer collection
collection.notFoundDescription: The collection link may be outdated or incomplete.

detail.notFoundDescription: The prayer link may be outdated or incomplete.
detail.textUnavailable: Text is not available for this language yet.

media.title: Prayer media
media.lyricsPlaceholderDescription: Lyrics will be added soon. For now, this page includes the zema video only.

about.title: About

hero.title: Tewahedo Daily
hero.description: A quiet corner of the internet for Ethiopian Orthodox Christians who want to stay close to the Church between Sundays - in minutes, not marathons.

whatThisSiteIs.title: What this website is
whatThisSiteIs.paragraph1: Tewahedo Daily is a daily companion: short lessons, practice for chants and movement, a dedicated prayers space, and a quick view of today in the Church calendar. It is built for phones and busy days so you can touch prayer and tradition without feeling buried in text.
whatThisSiteIs.paragraph2: We are not an encyclopedia or a social feed. We are trying to be faithful, small, and useful.

audience.title: Who this is for
audience.item1: Ethiopians in the diaspora - especially when parish life is far away or schedules are tight.
audience.item2: Beginners who need a gentle starting place without already knowing every hymn or rubric.
audience.item3: Anyone who loves the Church and wants a lighter rhythm between worship, work, and family.

features.title: Feature overview
features.intro: Four threads - each one optional. Pick what fits the day.

churchDay.title: The Church day
churchDay.description: A quick sense of what today is - feast, season, and a short why-it-matters summary.

chants.title: Chants
chants.description: Mezmur and werb together: listen, read transliteration, and open lyrics or watch only when you choose.

prayers.title: Prayers
prayers.description: Daily prayers, Wudase, and Mezmure Dawit under the prayer hub, with full text when you choose to open it.

calendar.title: Calendar and seasons
calendar.description: A simple calendar view and notes on fasts and feasts - always pointing back to your parish.

step1.title: Start on the home page.
step1.description: Open the home page and read Today in Church - one small moment with God is enough.

step2.title: Open Practice when you are ready.
step2.description: Use Practice for chants and tools, or Prayers for tselot and longer reading. Text stays in cards and dialogs until you tap.

step3.title: Return tomorrow.
step3.description: The site is meant to be repeated, not finished. Same gentle shape, fresh day.

guidance.title: Respect and guidance
guidance.paragraph1: This website supports learning and practice in daily life. We hope it encourages prayer, listening, and love for the Church.
guidance.paragraph2: It does not replace your priest, parish, spiritual father, or official liturgical books. Fasting rules, sacraments, confession, and pastoral care belong in person with those the Church has given you.
guidance.paragraph3: If something here disagrees with your bishop, books, or father of confession, trust them - not a website.

futureVision.title: Future vision
futureVision.description: We hope to grow with real calendar data, parish-tuned content, audio that serves the liturgy, and more - always slowly, carefully, and under proper guidance. If Tewahedo Daily ever stops feeling humble next to the Church, we have missed the mark.

about.closing: Thank you for visiting. May it serve your salvation - even a little.
